task: I need to text my supervisor that I can cover part of a shift but not the whole thing. Can you draft it in a way that’s clear and ready to send, without making it sound too stiff?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are a few ready-to-send options:

### Simple
**“I can cover part of the shift, but not the full thing. Let me know what part you need me for.”**

### A little warmer
**“I can help cover part of the shift, just not the whole thing. Let me know what time block would help most.”**

### More direct
**“I’m available for part of the shift, but I can’t stay for the full shift. Let me know what coverage you need.”**

Tiny version:
**“Can cover part, not all. Let me know what you need.”**

If you want, I can make it sound:
- more casual
- more professional
- more apologetic

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. The warm one sounds right — clear but not stiff.

I can help cover part of the shift, just not the whole thing. Let me know what time block would help most.
